description: Bundled cards for bleed-air de-ice boot operation, pneumatic pressure and icing terminology.

cards.front: What aircraft systems are operated by bleed air?
cards.back: Environmental system, de-ice boots, intake deflectors, and autopilot.

cards.front: What bleed air pressure is regulated for de-ice boot operation?
What is the maximum allowed bleed air pressure?
What is the complete boot cycle time in auto-fast mode?
cards.back: About 1 minute: 5-5-3-3 timing with 44 seconds off.

cards.front: What is the complete boot cycle time in auto-slow mode?
cards.back: About 3 minutes: 5-5-3-3 timing followed by 164 seconds off.

cards.front: What do two blue tail-boot lights indicate when equipped?
cards.back: Only that pressure is available for boot inflation.

cards.front: What does the pneumatic low-pressure light indicate?
cards.back: Insufficient pressure is available to inflate the boots.

cards.front: What is required for optional propeller boot operation?
cards.back: Electrical power and oil pressure above 40 PSI.

cards.front: How does the AFM define icing conditions?
cards.back: The presence of atmospheric moisture and temperature conducive to airplane icing.
Water drops that remain unfrozen below 0°C and may freeze on contact with aircraft surfaces.
